behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is at the model-output handoff boundary (embedding provider -> vector index schema), not just a pure local function. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP: This behavior depends on real AI/ML output shape from the embedding model. PROOF-BUNDLE: (1) Forcing phrase: "embedding vector produced for a given query string" and "correct dimensionality for the vector database index." (2) Lower-level miss: an Integration test with a stubbed embedding (fixed-length array) always matches the index dimension and misses real failures like model/version drift changing dimensions. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered, but rejected because the critical risk is model output characteristics, not OS/subprocess behavior; no multi-step agent pipeline, so Workflow is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model with representative queries, then validate the returned vector length exactly matches the live index dimension used by the retrieval path; fail if any mismatch occurs.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The behavior targets retrieval engine ordering at runtime, which can fail due to real DB/index configuration and ANN engine behavior. REAL-INFRA: Requires real vector DB behavior (service/process/networked engine), not just in-process mocks. PROOF-BUNDLE: (1) Forcing phrase: "similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score." (2) Lower-level miss: an Integration test with an in-memory fake store can return perfectly sorted fixtures and miss production misconfiguration (wrong metric/index settings) where real engine ordering differs. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic was considered but rejected because this behavior does not require judging AI output quality; it validates numeric ordering from similarity search.
behaviors.test_description: Run queries against the real vector database/index used in deployment (or an equivalent real engine instance), capture returned scores, and assert strict non-increasing cosine similarity ordering for top-K results.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is in the model’s ranking judgment; stubs cannot represent nuanced intent handling. LLM-DEP: Correctness explicitly depends on what a real LLM decides. PROOF-BUNDLE: (1) Forcing phrase: "LLM re-ranking step changes the order" and "when the query has nuanced intent." (2) Lower-level miss: Integration with a canned reranker response will always show expected reorder and miss cases where the real LLM keeps raw order or reorders incorrectly for nuance. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow was considered but rejected because this is a single agentic decision point, not multiple agentic stages chained together.
behaviors.test_description: For a curated set of nuance-heavy queries, run real retrieval + real LLM reranking and assert at least one position change versus raw similarity order, while logging unchanged cases as failures for this behavior.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The risk is semantic mismatch detection from real embeddings; synthetic vectors can mask real-world false positives. LLM-DEP (AI/ML dependency): Behavior depends on actual embedding model outputs for natural-language queries. PROOF-BUNDLE: (1) Forcing phrase: "query with no close matches" and "returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise." (2) Lower-level miss: Integration with handcrafted vectors/threshold fixtures can pass while real embedding space places unrelated queries near some items, producing noisy results. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because this behavior does not require multiple sequential model decisions; it is one retrieval decision boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Use real embedding generation and real retrieval on adversarial out-of-domain queries; assert zero returned items when top similarity is below production threshold, verifying no low-relevance fallback items leak through.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Latency failures emerge from real HTTP hops, model API latency, vector DB response time, and serialization overhead; mocks hide these. REAL-INFRA: This is explicitly an end-to-end timing SLO, requiring real runtime infrastructure. PROOF-BUNDLE: (1) Forcing phrase: "end-to-end latency ... below 2 seconds for the median case." (2) Lower-level miss: Integration with stubbed embedding/LLM calls can always pass timing while production exceeds 2s due to real network and provider latency distributions. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic was considered but rejected because assertion targets timing, not model output quality/decision correctness; Workflow rejected because this is a performance SLO, not correctness of multi-agent reasoning outcomes.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like HTTP requests through the full deployed stack with real external dependencies, collect latency over a representative sample, and assert p50 <= 2000 ms.
